description: Master schema defining the canonical structure of a CompatibilityRule in the Dynamic Compatibility and Configuration Compliance Engine. Governs rule extraction, validation, Neo4j graph generation, Qdrant vector generation, compliance execution, root cause analysis, and recommendation generation.

rule_id.name: rule_id
rule_id.description: Globally unique, stable identifier for this compatibility rule. Immutable after creation. Used as the primary key in Neo4j nodes, Qdrant payloads, and all cross-system references.
validation_constraints: Must match pattern ^CRULE-[A-Z0-9]+-[0-9]{3,}$
Must be unique across all rules in all lifecycle states
Must not be recycled or reassigned after any lifecycle transition
Must not contain spaces or lowercase characters

rule_type.name: rule_type
rule_type.description: Classification of the rule semantic type. Determines which validation logic, evaluation model, and required fields apply. Sourced from the compatibility_rule_types.json ontology.
validation_constraints: Must be one of the 6 registered rule types
Must not be null or empty
Determines minimum confidence threshold for the validation gate

status.name: status
status.description: Current lifecycle state of the rule. Controls production eligibility, import permissions, and allowed operations. Maps to the lifecycle state machine in compatibility_lifecycle.json.
validation_constraints: Must be one of the 7 defined lifecycle states
Only approved rules are eligible for Neo4j and Qdrant import
State transitions must follow the allowed transition matrix
Must not be null

subject.name: subject
subject.description: The primary entity the rule applies to. Represents the component being evaluated or constrained by this rule. Must reference a valid RC2 entity_id from the canonical entity registry.

entity_id.description: RC2 canonical entity identifier. Must match pattern ^[A-Z]{2,3}-[0-9]{3}$.

component_name.description: Human-readable canonical name of the subject entity (e.g., BIOS, Driver Pack).

version_constraint.description: Version constraint applied to the subject entity during rule evaluation.

version_scheme.description: Version scheme: semantic, wildcard, named_release, calendar, unknown

requirement_kind.description: Kind: min_version, max_version, exact_version, version_range, required_present, must_not_be_present, readiness

validation_constraints: entity_id must exist in the RC2 canonical entity registry
entity_id must match pattern ^[A-Z]{2,3}-[0-9]{3}$
component_name must not be null or empty
version_constraint.operator must be from the allowed operators for the rule_type
version_constraint.version_scheme must be one of: semantic, wildcard, named_release, calendar, unknown
subject.neo4j_mapping: Resolved via TARGETS relationship to :Entity node with matching entity_id

predicate.name: predicate
predicate.description: The semantic relationship being asserted between the subject and object entities. Must be drawn from the 20-term controlled vocabulary defined in compatibility_relationships.json.
validation_constraints: Must be one of the 20 registered relationship names
Must not be null or empty
Must be semantically consistent with the rule_type (e.g., FIXED_BY for known_issue_fixed)
predicate.neo4j_mapping: Relationship type on the primary edge :CompatibilityRule -[PREDICATE]-> :Entity

object.name: object
object.description: The secondary entity the rule asserts a relationship to. Same structure as subject. Represents the component that the predicate points to.

entity_id.description: RC2 canonical entity identifier. Must match pattern ^[A-Z]{2,3}-[0-9]{3}$.

component_name.description: Human-readable canonical name of the object entity.

version_constraint.description: Version constraint applied to the object entity.

validation_constraints: entity_id must exist in the RC2 canonical entity registry
entity_id must match pattern ^[A-Z]{2,3}-[0-9]{3}$
component_name must not be null or empty
object.entity_id must be different from subject.entity_id
object.neo4j_mapping: Target :Entity node referenced by the primary relationship edge

conditions.name: conditions
conditions.description: Array of conditions that must be satisfied (per condition_logic) for this rule to fire. Universal rules (applying to all contexts) use an empty array. Each condition scopes the rule to a specific system state. Minimum 2 conditions required for incompatible_combination rules.

condition_id.description: Unique identifier within this rule, e.g. COND-001

entity_id.description: RC2 entity_id of the entity being checked

version_scheme.description: Version scheme: semantic, wildcard, named_release, calendar, unknown

condition_context.description: Optional context label such as planned_migration_target, installed_state

validation_constraints: Can be empty array for universal rules (rule fires regardless of system context)
incompatible_combination rules must have at least 2 condition entries
Each condition_id must be unique within the conditions array
Each entity_id must exist in the RC2 registry
operator must be from the allowed set for the rule_type
conditions.neo4j_mapping: Array serialized as :Condition nodes connected via HAS_CONDITION relationships
conditions.qdrant_mapping: Serialized as JSON string in payload field conditions_json; entity IDs extracted as conditions_entity_ids (keyword array)

exceptions.name: exceptions
exceptions.description: Optional list of exceptions that suppress rule evaluation for specific device families, entities, or scope contexts. When a system matches an exception, the rule does not fire even if conditions are met.

exception_id.description: Unique exception identifier within this rule

scope_type.description: Type of scope: device_family, entity_id, platform_tag

excluded_entity_ids.description: RC2 entity IDs excluded from rule evaluation

excluded_device_families.description: Device family names or tags excluded from evaluation

justification.description: Human-readable explanation of why the exception was granted

examples.justification: Legacy rack servers are decommissioned and excluded from compliance policy.

validation_constraints: exception_id must be unique within the exceptions array
excluded_entity_ids values must exist in the RC2 registry if provided
justification must not be null or empty
scope_type must be one of: device_family, entity_id, platform_tag
exceptions.neo4j_mapping: Connected via HAS_EXCEPTION relationship to :Exception nodes
exceptions.qdrant_mapping: Not indexed in Qdrant by default; referenced in payload field has_exceptions (boolean)

dependencies.name: dependencies
dependencies.description: Optional list of other components or rules that must be in a certain state for this rule to be evaluable. Dependencies are soft prerequisites; missing dependencies generate a warning, not an evaluation failure.

dependency_id.description: Unique dependency identifier within this rule

entity_id.description: RC2 entity_id of the dependency entity

version_normalized.description: Version value for the dependency check

dependency_type.description: Type of dependency: requires_present, requires_version, prerequisite_rule

validation_constraints: dependency_id must be unique within the dependencies array
entity_id must exist in the RC2 registry
dependency_type must be one of: requires_present, requires_version, prerequisite_rule
dependencies.neo4j_mapping: Connected via DEPENDS_ON relationship to :Entity or :CompatibilityRule nodes
dependencies.qdrant_mapping: Not directly indexed; referenced in payload as dependency_count (integer)

conflicts.name: conflicts
conflicts.description: Optional list of known conflict entries associated with this rule. Conflicts document specific component versions or states that directly conflict with the rule subject. Distinct from incompatible_combination rule_type in that these are supplemental annotations to any rule type.

conflict_id.description: Unique conflict identifier within this rule

entity_id.description: RC2 entity_id of the conflicting entity

component_name.description: Human-readable name of the conflicting component

operator.description: Version comparison operator for the conflict range

conflict_symptom.description: Description of the failure mode or symptom if the conflict is present

examples.conflict_symptom: Boot failure or system hang when firmware is below 8.0.0

validation_constraints: conflict_id must be unique within the conflicts array
entity_id must exist in the RC2 registry
severity must be one of: critical, warning, info
conflict_symptom must not be null or empty
conflicts.neo4j_mapping: Connected via CONFLICTS_WITH relationship to :ConflictConstraint nodes
conflicts.qdrant_mapping: Payload field conflict_count (integer); conflict entity IDs as conflict_entity_ids (keyword array)

evidence.name: evidence
evidence.required: conditionally required — required when status = approved
evidence.description: Array of evidence records that provide source traceability for this rule. Required when status is approved. Each evidence entry links the rule to the source document chunk from which the rule was extracted.

source_type.description: Source evidence type: extracted_text, advisory, cve_record, test_result, manual_entry

source_chunk_id.description: Chunk identifier within the source document, pattern CHUNK-*

source_excerpt.description: Verbatim or near-verbatim excerpt from the source document supporting this rule

confidence_score.description: Confidence score for this evidence item, 0.0 to 1.0

extraction_method.description: Method used: llm_extraction, regex_pattern, manual_entry, advisory_parse

examples.source_excerpt: BIOS version 6.4.2 requires System Firmware 8.2.1 or later.
CVE / ID: SEC-2026-001 | Fixed In: BIOS Version 6.4.2

validation_constraints: Required when status = approved (minimum 1 evidence entry)
evidence_id must match pattern EV-[A-Z0-9-]+-[0-9]{3}
source_type must be one of: extracted_text, advisory, cve_record, test_result, manual_entry
source_document_id must match pattern DOC-[A-Z0-9]+
confidence_score must be in range 0.0 to 1.0
source_excerpt must not be null or empty for approved rules
extraction_method must be one of: llm_extraction, regex_pattern, manual_entry, advisory_parse
evidence.neo4j_mapping: Connected via HAS_EVIDENCE relationship to :Evidence nodes with source_document_id linking to :CompatibilityDocument
evidence.qdrant_mapping: source_excerpt is the primary embedding payload text for semantic search; evidence_count (integer) in payload metadata

remediations.name: remediations
remediations.description: Array of remediation steps that resolve the rule violation. Required for all rules. Provides actionable upgrade paths and fix instructions for the recommendation engine.

remediation_id.description: Unique remediation identifier within this rule, e.g. REM-001

remediation_type.description: Type: upgrade_component, remove_component, configuration_change, install_component, manual_action

target_entity_id.description: RC2 entity_id of the component to remediate

target_component_name.description: Human-readable name of the target component

target_version.description: Target version to upgrade to or install

operator.description: Version operator for the remediation target (>=, ==)

sequence_order.description: Order of this remediation step within a multi-step remediation plan

remediation_hint.description: Human-readable instruction for executing this remediation step

examples.remediation_hint: Upgrade System Firmware to version 8.2.1 or later before applying BIOS update.
Upgrade Driver Pack to 12.5.0 to satisfy Enterprise OS 2026.1 readiness requirement.

validation_constraints: At least 1 remediation entry required
remediation_id must be unique within the remediations array
target_entity_id must exist in the RC2 registry
remediation_type must be one of: upgrade_component, remove_component, configuration_change, install_component, manual_action
remediation_hint must not be null or empty
sequence_order must be a positive integer if provided
remediations.neo4j_mapping: Connected via HAS_REMEDIATION relationship to :Remediation nodes
remediations.qdrant_mapping: remediation_hint text included in document payload for semantic matching; remediation_count (integer) in metadata

source_document.name: source_document
source_document.description: Document identifier of the source compatibility document from which this rule was extracted. Must match the DOC-* pattern used in the compatibility document registry.
validation_constraints: Must match pattern ^DOC-[A-Z0-9]+$
Must reference a registered source document in the document registry
Must not be null or empty
source_document.neo4j_mapping: Node property :CompatibilityRule {source_document}; REFERENCES relationship to :CompatibilityDocument node

source_section.name: source_section
source_section.description: Optional reference to the specific section, chunk, page, or heading within the source document where this rule was found. Improves traceability for human review and audit.
validation_constraints: If provided, must not be an empty string
Recommended format: CHUNK-NNNNNN, Section X.Y, or Page N

confidence.name: confidence
confidence.description: Overall confidence score for this rule, representing the extraction and validation certainty. Minimum thresholds vary by rule_type: min_version_constraint >= 0.70, known_issue_fixed >= 0.80, readiness_requirement >= 0.70, feature_support_added >= 0.70, incompatible_combination >= 0.85, update_order_constraint >= 0.85.
validation_constraints: Must be a decimal number in range 0.0 to 1.0 inclusive
Must meet the minimum threshold for the declared rule_type
Must not be null

severity.name: severity
severity.description: Business impact severity of a rule violation. Drives alert priority and remediation urgency in the compliance engine. Allowed values depend on rule_type.
validation_constraints: Must be one of: critical, warning, info
feature_support_added rules must not use severity critical
incompatible_combination and update_order_constraint rules must not use severity info
Must not be null

condition_logic.name: condition_logic
condition_logic.description: Logical operator applied across all entries in the conditions array. AND means all conditions must be true for the rule to fire. OR means any one condition being true causes the rule to fire.
validation_constraints: Must be AND or OR
Must not be null
Default value is AND
Must be AND or OR in uppercase

sequence_step.name: sequence_step
sequence_step.required: conditionally required — required when rule_type = update_order_constraint
sequence_step.description: The step number of this rule within a sequenced upgrade chain. Must be >= 1. Required when rule_type is update_order_constraint.
validation_constraints: Required when rule_type = update_order_constraint
Must be an integer >= 1
Must be less than or equal to sequence_total_steps if that field is present
Must not be null for update_order_constraint rules

sequence_total_steps.name: sequence_total_steps
sequence_total_steps.description: Optional total number of steps in the sequenced upgrade chain. Helps compliance engines and UIs present the full sequence to the operator. Must be >= 2 if provided.
validation_constraints: Must be an integer >= 2 if provided
Must be >= sequence_step if both are present
Applicable primarily to update_order_constraint rules

sequence_prerequisite_ref.name: sequence_prerequisite_ref
sequence_prerequisite_ref.required: conditionally required — required when rule_type = update_order_constraint and sequence_step > 1
sequence_prerequisite_ref.description: The rule_id of the preceding rule in the sequence chain. Enables chain traversal for root cause analysis. Must reference an existing rule_id. Required for update_order_constraint rules where sequence_step > 1.
validation_constraints: Required when rule_type = update_order_constraint and sequence_step > 1
Must match the CRULE-* rule_id pattern
Must reference an existing rule_id in the registry
Circular references are forbidden
Must not self-reference
sequence_prerequisite_ref.neo4j_mapping: REFERENCES relationship pointing to preceding :CompatibilityRule node in the sequence chain

issue_id.name: issue_id
issue_id.description: Issue or CVE identifier associated with a known_issue_fixed rule. Provides a direct link to the security advisory, bug report, or known issue record. Optional for other rule types but recommended for security-related rules.
validation_constraints: If provided, must be a non-empty string
Recommended for all known_issue_fixed rules
Format: SEC-YYYY-NNN, CVE-YYYY-NNNNN, KI-NNN, or BUG-NNNN

tags.name: tags
tags.description: Optional classification tags for the rule. Used to filter, group, and prioritize rules in the compliance engine and dashboards. Common tag values include unverified_value, security_critical, eol_component, pending_vendor_confirmation, high_impact.
validation_constraints: Each tag must be a non-empty string
Tags must not contain spaces (use underscores)
Maximum 10 tags per rule recommended

created_timestamp.name: created_timestamp
created_timestamp.description: UTC timestamp when this rule was first created in the system. Set automatically by the extraction pipeline. Must not be modified after creation.
validation_constraints: Must be a valid ISO-8601 datetime string
Must include timezone designator (Z for UTC or +HH:MM offset)
Must not be null
Must not be modified after initial creation

updated_timestamp.name: updated_timestamp
updated_timestamp.description: UTC timestamp when this rule was last modified. Updated automatically on every field change. Used for change tracking and audit trail continuity.
validation_constraints: Must be a valid ISO-8601 datetime string
Must include timezone designator
Must not be null
Must be >= created_timestamp

approved_by.name: approved_by
approved_by.required: conditionally required — required when status = approved
approved_by.description: Identifier of the human reviewer who approved this rule for production use. Must be populated before a rule can enter the approved state. Format is reviewer ID or username.
validation_constraints: Required when status = approved
Must be a non-empty string when required
Must be null or absent when status is not approved
Must be set simultaneously with approved_at
approved_by.neo4j_mapping: Node property :CompatibilityRule {approved_by}; APPROVED_BY relationship to :Reviewer node

approved_at.name: approved_at
approved_at.required: conditionally required — required when status = approved
approved_at.description: UTC timestamp when this rule was formally approved. Must be populated simultaneously with approved_by. Required when status is approved.
validation_constraints: Required when status = approved
Must be a valid ISO-8601 datetime string when required
Must include timezone designator
Must be >= created_timestamp
Must be set simultaneously with approved_by
